summary: test_create_non_review_tasks_are_unaffected_by_review_validation exercises real TaskService calls (not mocked) covering both boundary disjuncts: a non-review 'spike' task created without a governor_task_id (would raise if a naive impl over-broadly required a governor) and one created with a governor plus an equal harness string 'claude'==‘claude' (would raise if the harness-inequality check weren't gated to workflow=='review'); no pytest.raises wraps either call, so any erroneous rejection surfaces as an uncaught exception, and confirmed via  that today's absence of REQ-001.1/1.2 implementation makes this pass non-trivially meaningful for the negative-space check.
